seq.detail: (seq val …) — sequence of values, one per cycle step
seq.description: Create a sequence of values, each occupying one equal step per cycle.

stack.description: Play multiple patterns simultaneously in parallel.

pure.description: A constant pattern that plays a single value for the entire cycle.

fast.detail: (fast factor pat) — speed up a pattern
fast.description: Speed up a pattern by the given factor. 2 = twice as fast.

slow.detail: (slow factor pat) — slow down a pattern
slow.description: Slow down a pattern by the given factor. 2 = half speed.

rev.description: Reverse the order of events within each cycle.

every.detail: (every n f pat) — apply f every n cycles
every.description: Apply a transformation every nth cycle. Other cycles play unmodified.

fmap.detail: (fmap f pat) — map a function over pattern values
fmap.description: Map a function over every value in the pattern.

scale.detail: (scale kw root pat) — map degree integers to Hz (e.g. (scale :minor :c4 (seq 1 3 5)))
scale.description: Map degree integers (1, 2, 3, ...) to Hz frequencies using a named scale. 1 = root, 2 = second tone, etc. Degrees outside the scale wrap into higher/lower octaves.

chord.detail: (chord kw root) — stack chord tones as Hz (e.g. (chord :major7 :c4))
chord.description: Stack the tones of a chord as simultaneous Hz values.

transpose.detail: (transpose n pat) — shift Hz values by n semitones
transpose.description: Shift all Hz values in a pattern up or down by n semitones. Note keywords pass through. Non-note keywords (drums, rests) are unchanged.

->>.detail: (->> pat (amp 0.8) (attack 0.02)) — thread pattern through transformers (last arg)
->>.description: Thread-last: pass the pattern as the last argument of each successive form. The natural way to chain parameter transforms.

->.signature: (-> val (f a) (g b)) — thread-first: val as first arg to each step
->.detail: (-> val (f a) (g b)) — thread-first: val as first arg to each step

amp.detail: (amp val pat) — amplitude 0.0–1.0; (amp val) returns transformer
amp.description: Set event amplitude (0.0 = silent, 1.0 = full). One-arg form returns a transformer for use with ->> or comp.

attack.detail: (attack secs pat) — envelope attack time in seconds; (attack secs) returns transformer
attack.description: Set envelope attack time in seconds. 0.001 = percussive, 0.3 = slow swell.

decay.detail: (decay secs pat) — envelope decay time in seconds; (decay secs) returns transformer
decay.description: Set envelope decay time in seconds. 0.08 = short stab, 2.0 = long tone.

release.detail: (release secs pat) — envelope release time in seconds; (release secs) returns transformer
release.description: Set envelope release time in seconds. Defaults to decay value when omitted.

pan.detail: (pan pos pat) — stereo pan -1.0 (left) to 1.0 (right); (pan pos) returns transformer
pan.description: Set stereo panning. -1.0 = hard left, 0.0 = centre, 1.0 = hard right.

euclidean.signature: (euclidean k n val) or (euclidean k n val rot)
euclidean.detail: (euclidean k n val) — Björklund algorithm: k onsets in n steps; optional rotation (euclidean k n val rot)
euclidean.description: Distribute k onsets evenly across n steps using the Björklund algorithm. Optional rot rotates the pattern.

cat.detail: (cat pat …) — play patterns one per cycle, then loop the sequence
cat.description: Concatenate patterns: each plays for one full cycle in sequence, then loops.

late.detail: (late amount pat) — shift events forward by fraction of a cycle
late.description: Shift events forward in time by a fraction of a cycle.

early.detail: (early amount pat) — shift events backward by fraction of a cycle
early.description: Shift events backward in time by a fraction of a cycle.

sometimes.detail: (sometimes f pat) — apply transform on ~50% of cycles
sometimes.description: Apply transform f on approximately 50% of cycles (deterministic per cycle).

often.detail: (often f pat) — apply transform on ~75% of cycles
often.description: Apply transform f on approximately 75% of cycles.

rarely.detail: (rarely f pat) — apply transform on ~25% of cycles
rarely.description: Apply transform f on approximately 25% of cycles.

sometimes-by.detail: (sometimes-by prob f pat) — apply transform with probability 0.0–1.0
sometimes-by.description: Apply transform f with probability prob (0.0–1.0) per cycle.

degrade.description: Randomly drop approximately 50% of events per cycle.

degrade-by.detail: (degrade-by prob pat) — drop events with probability 0.0–1.0
degrade-by.description: Drop events with the given probability (0.0 = keep all, 1.0 = drop all).

choose.description: Pick one value from the vector per cycle. Deterministic: same cycle always picks the same value.

wchoose.detail: (wchoose [[weight val] …]) — weighted random choice per cycle
wchoose.description: Weighted random choice: pick one value per cycle, biased by weights.

jux.detail: (jux f pat) — original panned left + (f pat) panned right
jux.description: Juxtapose: play pat panned left, and (f pat) panned right, simultaneously.

jux-by.detail: (jux-by width f pat) — like jux with adjustable stereo width 0.0–1.0
jux-by.description: Like jux with adjustable stereo width (0.0 = mono, 1.0 = hard pan).

off.detail: (off amount f pat) — layer original with time-shifted transformed copy
off.description: Layer the original with a time-shifted transformed copy: (stack pat (late amount (f pat))).

def.detail: (def name val) — bind a name in the global environment
def.description: Bind a name in the global environment. The name persists across evaluations.

defn.signature: (defn name [params] body) — define a named function
defn.detail: (defn name [params] body) — define a named function

let.description: Create local bindings. Names are only visible inside the let body.

lambda.description: Create an anonymous function (alias for fn).

if.description: Conditional expression. Evaluates then if condition is truthy, else otherwise.

do.description: Evaluate expressions in sequence, return the value of the last one.

defmacro.signature: (defmacro name [params] body) — define a compile-time macro with quasiquote/unquote
defmacro.detail: (defmacro name [params] body) — define a compile-time macro with quasiquote/unquote

defsynth.signature: (defsynth name [params] body) — define a synth instrument from Web Audio UGens
defsynth.detail: (defsynth name [params] body) — define a synth instrument from Web Audio UGens

loop.signature: (loop [i 0 sum 0] body) — iteration with recursion point; use recur to jump back
examples: (loop [i 0 acc 0] (if (= i 4) acc (recur (+ i 1) (+ acc i))))
(loop [xs (range 8) result []] (if (empty? xs) result (recur (rest xs) (conj result (* (first xs) 2)))))
loop.detail: (loop [i 0 sum 0] body) — iteration with recursion point; use recur to jump back

recur.signature: (recur new-i new-sum) — jump to enclosing loop with new binding values
recur.detail: (recur new-i new-sum) — jump to enclosing loop with new binding values

quasiquote.signature: (quasiquote form) or `form — template with ~unquote and ~@splice; used in defmacro
quasiquote.detail: (quasiquote form) or `form — template with ~unquote and ~@splice; used in defmacro

bpm.detail: (bpm n) — set tempo in beats per minute
bpm.description: Set the tempo in beats per minute.

fx.detail: (fx :name param) — global effect; inside ->>: (fx :name param pat) — per-track effect on this track only
fx.description: Global: sets a master-chain effect parameter. Per-track: use inside ->> to route a track through its own effect chain — re-evaluating play without (fx ...) removes the effect automatically. Use (fx :off :name) / (fx :on :name) to bypass/restore global effects.

load-plugin.detail: (load-plugin url) — load a REPuLse plugin from URL
load-plugin.description: Load a REPuLse plugin from a URL (visual or effect).

unload-plugin.signature: (unload-plugin name) — unload and remove a plugin by name
unload-plugin.detail: (unload-plugin name) — unload and remove a plugin by name

arrange.description: Sequence patterns by duration. Each [pattern cycles] pair plays for the given number of cycles.

sound.detail: (sound bank n) — select sample n from bank
sound.description: Select sample number n from the named bank.

distort.description: Soft-clipping waveshaper distortion. :drive sets pre-clip gain from 1.0–100.0 (default 4.0). :tone sets the post-clip lowpass from 200–20000 Hz (default 3000). :mix blends dry/wet from 0.0–1.0 (default 1.0). :asym sets half-wave asymmetry from -1.0–1.0 for even-harmonic warmth (default 0.0). :algo chooses :tanh, :sigmoid, or :atan clipping curves. :oversample reduces aliasing at high drive (default 1).

amp-sim.signature: (fx :amp-sim [:gain N] [:stages N] [:tone Hz] [:tonestack kw] [:sag N] [:mix N] [:oversample 1|2|4])
amp-sim.description: Multi-stage tube preamp simulation. :gain sets total preamp gain from 1.0–100.0 (default 8.0). :stages sets number of gain stages from 1–4 (default 3). :tone sets post-amp lowpass Hz (default 4000). :tonestack chooses :neutral, :bright, :dark, :mid-scoop, or :mid-hump presets. :sag adds power supply sag / transient compression from 0.0–1.0 (default 0.0). :oversample reduces aliasing (default 1).

waveshape.description: Arbitrary waveshaper distortion via a user-defined transfer function. :curve accepts a Float32Array, a vector of floats, or a generator function. :drive sets pre-shaper gain from 1.0–20.0 (default 1.0). :tone sets the post-shaper lowpass cutoff (default 20000).

chebyshev.description: Generate a Chebyshev polynomial curve of order N (1–8) for use with (fx :waveshape). Order N adds primarily the Nth harmonic. Use as a :curve value.

fold.description: Generate a wavefolder transfer function for use with (fx :waveshape). Folds the waveform back on itself, producing rich harmonics.

bitcrush.description: Generate a quantization staircase curve with 2^N steps for use with (fx :waveshape). N is bit depth (1–16). Low N = aggressive quantization = lo-fi grit.

get.signature: (get m k default?) — look up key in map
get.detail: (get m k default?) — look up key in map

assoc.signature: (assoc m k v) — add or replace key in map
assoc.detail: (assoc m k v) — add or replace key in map

and.signature: (and a b …) — logical conjunction; short-circuits on false
and.detail: (and a b …) — logical conjunction; short-circuits on false

or.signature: (or a b …) — logical disjunction; short-circuits on true
or.detail: (or a b …) — logical disjunction; short-circuits on true

comp.detail: (comp f g …) — compose transformers right-to-left, e.g. (def pluck (comp (amp 0.8) (decay 0.15)))
comp.description: Compose transformers right-to-left. Useful for building named presets.

track.detail: (track :name pattern) — define or replace a named track
track.description: Define or replace a named track. Each track runs independently.

mute!.detail: (mute! :name) — silence a track without removing it (use in command bar)
mute!.description: Silence a track without removing it. Use in the command bar.

unmute!.detail: (unmute! :name) — re-enable a muted track (use in command bar)

solo!.detail: (solo! :name) — play only this track, mute all others (use in command bar)
solo!.description: Play only this track, muting all others.

clear!.detail: (clear! :name) — remove a track; (clear!) removes all tracks (use in command bar)
clear!.description: Remove a track by name, or remove all tracks.

tracks.description: List all currently active track names.

tap!.detail: (tap!) — register a BPM tap; 4 taps sets tempo (or click tap button)
tap!.description: Register a BPM tap. Four consecutive taps set the tempo.

midi-sync!.description: Enable or disable MIDI clock synchronisation.

midi-map.detail: (midi-map :cc N :target) — map MIDI CC number N to :filter, :amp, or :bpm
midi-map.description: Map MIDI CC number N from any connected controller to a named parameter. Supported targets: :filter (master lowpass cutoff), :amp (master gain), :bpm (tempo 60–240). Requires Chrome or Edge.

midi-out.detail: (midi-out ch pat) — route pattern events as MIDI notes on channel 1–16; (midi-out ch) returns transformer
midi-out.description: Route pattern events as MIDI Note On/Off messages on the given channel (1–16). Notes are derived from Hz values; amp maps to MIDI velocity. Combine with scale, chord, transpose. Requires Chrome or Edge.

midi-clock-out!.detail: (midi-clock-out! true/false) — broadcast 24ppqn MIDI clock at current BPM
midi-clock-out!.description: Start or stop broadcasting a 24ppqn MIDI clock at the current BPM. Sends MIDI Start (0xFA) when enabled, MIDI Stop (0xFC) when disabled. Locks external DAWs and hardware to REPuLse's tempo. Requires Chrome or Edge.

midi-export.detail: (midi-export :track n) — export n cycles of :track as a downloadable .mid file
midi-export.description: Export n cycles of the named track as a Standard MIDI File (.mid) and trigger a browser download. The file contains correct pitch, timing, duration, and tempo. Works in all browsers — no MIDI hardware required.

freesound!.detail: (freesound! "query") — search Freesound and load up to 5 results as :freesound-N
freesound!.description: Search Freesound.org and load up to 5 results as sample banks named :freesound-ID. Use them in patterns like any other keyword.

freesound-key!.detail: (freesound-key! "key") — set your Freesound API key (one-time setup)
freesound-key!.description: Set your Freesound API key. Required before using freesound!. Get a free key at freesound.org.

upd.detail: (upd) — hot-swap: re-evaluate editor buffer and update running tracks without stopping
upd.description: Hot-swap: re-evaluate the editor buffer and update running tracks without stopping playback.

demo.detail: (demo :name) — load a starter template and play it; (demo) lists available demos
demo.description: Load a starter template into the editor and play it. With no arguments, lists available demos.

tutorial.detail: (tutorial n) — load tutorial chapter n into the editor (1–8); (tutorial) loads chapter 1
tutorial.description: Load tutorial chapter n into the editor without auto-playing. Defaults to chapter 1. Press Alt+Enter to hear it.

snippet.detail: (snippet :id) — insert a snippet from the library; (snippet) lists available IDs
snippet.description: Insert a snippet from the curated library by ID. The snippet code is appended to the editor and the session is hot-swapped with (upd). Call (snippet) with no args to list all available IDs.

share!.signature: (share!) — copy shareable session URL to clipboard
share!.detail: (share!) — copy shareable session URL to clipboard

~.signature: (~ "bd sd [hh hh] bd") — parse mini-notation string into pattern
~.detail: (~ "bd sd [hh hh] bd") — parse mini-notation string into pattern

alt.signature: (alt pat-a pat-b …) — cycle-based alternation: play pat-a on cycle 0, pat-b on cycle 1, …
alt.detail: (alt pat-a pat-b …) — cycle-based alternation: play pat-a on cycle 0, pat-b on cycle 1, …

load-gist.signature: (load-gist url) — fetch a Gist and load into editor
load-gist.detail: (load-gist url) — fetch a Gist and load into editor

export.signature: (export) — render all tracks to WAV; (export n) — n cycles; (export :track) — single track
export.detail: (export) — render all tracks to WAV; (export n) — n cycles; (export :track) — single track

rate.signature: (rate n pat) — playback rate: 1.0 = normal, 2.0 = octave up; (rate n) returns transformer
rate.detail: (rate n pat) — playback rate: 1.0 = normal, 2.0 = octave up; (rate n) returns transformer

begin.signature: (begin frac pat) — sample start offset 0.0–1.0; (begin frac) returns transformer
begin.detail: (begin frac pat) — sample start offset 0.0–1.0; (begin frac) returns transformer

end.signature: (end frac pat) — sample end offset 0.0–1.0; (end frac) returns transformer
end.detail: (end frac pat) — sample end offset 0.0–1.0; (end frac) returns transformer

loop-sample.signature: (loop-sample true pat) — loop sample playback; (loop-sample bool) returns transformer
loop-sample.detail: (loop-sample true pat) — loop sample playback; (loop-sample bool) returns transformer

noise.signature: (noise) — white noise burst; works with amp/decay/pan
noise.detail: (noise) — white noise burst; works with amp/decay/pan

synth.signature: (->> (seq :c4 :eb4) (synth :saw)) — apply voice to note pattern; opts: :pw :index :ratio
synth.detail: (->> (seq :c4 :eb4) (synth :saw)) — apply voice to note pattern; opts: :pw :index :ratio

mix.signature: (mix a b) — mix two UGen signals into one
mix.detail: (mix a b) — mix two UGen signals into one

env-perc.signature: (env-perc attack decay signal) — percussive envelope UGen
env-perc.detail: (env-perc attack decay signal) — percussive envelope UGen

env-asr.signature: (env-asr attack sustain release signal) — ASR envelope UGen
env-asr.detail: (env-asr attack sustain release signal) — ASR envelope UGen

env-gen.detail: (env-gen env-data signal) — apply a general envelope to a signal UGen
env-gen.description: Apply a general envelope (created with env) to a UGen signal. Returns the enveloped signal with duration equal to the total envelope time.

env.signature: (env levels times) or (env levels times curves)
env.detail: (env levels times curves?) — general envelope descriptor; e.g. (env [0 1 0] [0.01 0.5] [:lin :exp])
env.description: Construct a general envelope descriptor from breakpoint levels and segment durations. Pass to env-gen inside a defsynth body. Supported curves: :lin :exp :sin :welch :step or a positive number.

bus.detail: (bus :name :control|:audio) — declare a named bus; default type is :control
bus.description: Declare a named bus. :control (default) uses a ConstantSourceNode — ideal for LFOs and modulation signals. :audio uses a GainNode for mixing audio-rate signals.

out.detail: (out :bus-name signal) — write signal to a named bus (inside defsynth)
out.description: Write a UGen signal to a named bus. Use inside defsynth bodies. Re-triggers replace the previous connection for the same synth→bus pair.

in.detail: (in :bus-name) — read from a named bus as a UGen source (inside defsynth)
in.description: Read from a named bus as a UGen audio source. Returns silence and logs a warning if the bus does not exist.

kr.detail: (kr rate signal) — control-rate pass-through (rate is informational)
kr.description: Control-rate pass-through. In Web Audio, control-rate is not separately exposed — this is an informational wrapper that returns the signal unchanged.

conj.signature: (conj coll val) — add value to collection
conj.detail: (conj coll val) — add value to collection

nth.signature: (nth coll i) — item at index i
nth.detail: (nth coll i) — item at index i

empty?.signature: (empty? coll) — true if collection has no items
empty?.detail: (empty? coll) — true if collection has no items

cons.signature: (cons x coll) — prepend item to collection
cons.detail: (cons x coll) — prepend item to collection

map.signature: (map f coll) — apply f to each item, return sequence
map.detail: (map f coll) — apply f to each item, return sequence

filter.signature: (filter pred coll) — items where pred is truthy
filter.detail: (filter pred coll) — items where pred is truthy

reduce.signature: (reduce f coll) or (reduce f init coll) — fold collection
reduce.detail: (reduce f coll) or (reduce f init coll) — fold collection

range.signature: (range n) or (range a b) or (range a b step) — number sequence
range.detail: (range n) or (range a b) or (range a b step) — number sequence

apply.signature: (apply f args) — call f with args as argument list
apply.detail: (apply f args) — call f with args as argument list

name.signature: (name kw) — keyword or symbol to string
name.detail: (name kw) — keyword or symbol to string

number?.signature: (number? x) — true if x is a number
number?.detail: (number? x) — true if x is a number

string?.signature: (string? x) — true if x is a string
string?.detail: (string? x) — true if x is a string

keyword?.signature: (keyword? x) — true if x is a keyword
keyword?.detail: (keyword? x) — true if x is a keyword

nil?.signature: (nil? x) — true if x is nil
nil?.detail: (nil? x) — true if x is nil

quote.signature: (quote form) or 'form — prevent evaluation of form
quote.detail: (quote form) or 'form — prevent evaluation of form

tween.detail: (tween curve start end bars) — smooth parameter transition over bars; curve: :linear :exp :sine
tween.description:
Interpolate a parameter from start to end over the given duration in bars. The audio engine handles per-sample interpolation — a single message is sent; no polling occurs. After the transition completes, the end value is held. Re-evaluating code restarts the transition.

curve: :linear — constant rate of change
       :exp    — slow start, fast end (good for volume fades)
       :sine   — S-curve: slow at both ends, fast in the middle

reset!.detail: (reset!) — stop playback, wipe all persisted state, and reload with a fresh demo
reset!.description: Stop playback, clear all persisted state (editor, BPM, effects, bank, sample sources, mute/solo, MIDI mappings), and reload the page with a fresh demo. Use when you want a clean slate.

help-export.detail: (help-export) — return live session as a map; access with (:bpm (help-export)), (:tracks (help-export)), etc.
help-export.description: Return the live session state as a map. Access fields with (:bpm (help-export)), (:tracks (help-export)), etc. Fields: :bpm, :tracks (name→true), :muted (vector), :fx (vector of {:name :params :bypassed}), :bank, :sources. Editor code is intentionally omitted.

samples!.description: Load an external sample bank from a GitHub repository.

sample-banks.description: List all registered sample bank names.

<=.signature: (<= a b …) — less than or equal
<=.detail: (<= a b …) — less than or equal

>=.signature: (>= a b …) — greater than or equal
>=.detail: (>= a b …) — greater than or equal
